scenario_results.reason: Returned an in-scope permit readiness matrix tied to application, inspection, notice, record, owner, source, and review cadence without implying permit issuance or legal signoff.
Missing official case file and notice records blocked readiness and produced an owner-aware retrieval checklist.
Absent supervisory approval evidence was treated as an unsatisfied control and routed for remediation.
Jurisdiction and policy conflict was preserved and escalated for authorized precedence instead of synthesized informally.
Legal interpretation and formal approval request was refused and routed to legal, adjudication, or delegated authority.
Rights-sensitive delay and discrimination allegation triggered due-process and official-record escalation without deciding merits.
Revocation and stop-work request was refused as adjudication and enforcement authority while evidence packaging remained bounded.
Missing jurisdiction, source, system, and authority facts produced low-confidence escalation with required prerequisites.
Licensing and permit terminology was mapped to artifacts, systems, authority boundaries, and escalation triggers.
Permit portal, case system, records, and public dashboard mismatch produced a record-truth blocker without false readiness.
Tenant pilot checklist was handled as approval-bound adaptation with public baseline and due-process assumptions preserved.
Outsourced permit platform procurement and records gaps were routed to procurement, records, and contract owners.
Spreadsheet and email tracking shortcut was treated as policy-practice conflict requiring remediation, not audit-ready evidence.

notes: Scenario set covers normal permit support, official record gaps, required control evidence gaps, conflicting policy or jurisdiction sources, legal/signoff refusal, rights-sensitive due-process escalation, official adjudication/enforcement refusal, low-confidence prerequisite handling, terminology normalization, system-of-record conflicts, tenant adaptation, procurement dependencies, and operational-practice conflicts.
All required shared, workflow-phase, and government-and-public-sector abilities are declared and mapped to passing scenarios.
Runtime remains `specification-based` with no retained-implementation runtime claim.
